---
title: "Ferrari Championship Trajectory"
subtitle: "Does Ferrari consistently squander strong championship positions?"
---

## The narrative

> *"Ferrari always bottles it — they consistently squander strong championship positions
> as the season progresses."*

The full version of this hypothesis (championship lead conversion rate across all seasons)
requires historical data beyond our 2022–2026 scope. For the available data, the reframed
question is: **in seasons where Ferrari were competitive, when does the gap to the eventual
champion start widening, and why?**

---

## Method

Cumulative constructor points are tracked round by round. The gap to the championship leader
is computed at each round. Seasons analysed: **2022** (Ferrari led early) and **2024**
(Ferrari competitive throughout but ultimately second).

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '../..')

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import scripts.export_data as exp

gap_df = exp.read('championship_trajectory')

FERRARI_SEASONS = [2022, 2024]
ferrari = gap_df[
    (gap_df['constructor_id'] == 'ferrari') &
    (gap_df['season'].isin(FERRARI_SEASONS))
].copy()

# Top 3 competitors per season for context
top_constructors = (
    gap_df[gap_df['season'].isin(FERRARI_SEASONS)]
    .groupby(['season', 'constructor_id'])['cumulative_points']
    .max()
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values(['season', 'cumulative_points'], ascending=[True, False])
    .groupby('season')
    .head(4)
)
```

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Ferrari's cumulative gap to the championship leader by round. Zero = Ferrari are leading; positive = points behind the leader."

SEASON_COLOURS = {2022: '#E8002D', 2024: '#FF6B35'}
COMPETITOR_COLOURS = ['#636EFA', '#00CC96', '#AB63FA', '#FFA15A']

fig = go.Figure()

for season in FERRARI_SEASONS:
    s = ferrari[ferrari['season'] == season].sort_values('round')
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=s['round'],
        y=s['gap_to_leader'],
        mode='lines+markers',
        name=f'Ferrari {season}',
        line=dict(color=SEASON_COLOURS[season], width=3),
        marker=dict(size=6),
        hovertemplate=(
            f'<b>Ferrari {season}</b><br>'
            'Round %{x}<br>'
            'Gap to leader: %{y} pts<extra></extra>'
        ),
    ))

    # Add top competitors for context (excluding Ferrari itself)
    season_top = top_constructors[
        (top_constructors['season'] == season) &
        (top_constructors['constructor_id'] != 'ferrari')
    ]['constructor_id'].tolist()

    for j, cid in enumerate(season_top[:2]):
        comp = gap_df[
            (gap_df['season'] == season) &
            (gap_df['constructor_id'] == cid)
        ].sort_values('round')
        cname = comp['constructor_name'].iloc[0] if len(comp) else cid
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=comp['round'],
            y=comp['gap_to_leader'],
            mode='lines',
            name=f'{cname} {season}',
            line=dict(color=COMPETITOR_COLOURS[j], width=1.5, dash='dot'),
            opacity=0.6,
            hovertemplate=(
                f'<b>{cname} {season}</b><br>'
                'Round %{x}<br>'
                'Gap to leader: %{y} pts<extra></extra>'
            ),
        ))

fig.update_layout(
    title='Ferrari Championship Gap to Leader — 2022 & 2024',
    xaxis_title='Round',
    yaxis_title='Points behind leader',
    yaxis=dict(autorange='reversed'),
    template='plotly_dark',
    legend=dict(
        orientation='h',
        yanchor='top',
        y=-0.15,
        xanchor='center',
        x=0.5,
    ),
    height=540,
    margin=dict(b=120),
)
fig.show()
```

---

## Findings

| Question | Finding |
|---|---|
| In 2022, at which round did Ferrari's gap start widening? | Round 5 — but this followed early Red Bull reliability failures; Ferrari led the championship to that point |
| In 2024, at which round did Ferrari's gap start widening? | Round 3, then again around Round 7–8; Ferrari recovered from Round 18 onward |
| How does Ferrari's decline compare to other competitors? | 2022: similar to Mercedes as Red Bull pulled away from the entire field; 2024: Ferrari were second-best for most of the season behind Red Bull, then eventual winners McLaren |
| Is there a consistent pattern? | No — the two seasons show very different trajectories |

**Verdict: Not supported — on two seasons of data.**

In 2022, Ferrari's gap widened because Red Bull achieved dominant pace after early reliability
issues — the same happened to every other constructor. In 2024, Ferrari were genuinely
competitive and finished second in the Constructors' Championship. Neither season supports
the "bottles it" narrative in isolation. Two seasons is directional only.

**Deferred:** The full championship lead conversion rate analysis — across seasons where
Ferrari led at mid-season — requires pre-2022 historical data. See v0.7.0 backlog.
